segments: كَانَ دَوَاءُ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) السَّعْتَرَ وَ كَانَ يَقُولُ إِنَّهُ يَصِيرُ لِلْمَعِدَةِ خَمْلًا كَخَمْلِ الْقَطِيفَةِ .
arabic.text: كَانَ دَوَاءُ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) السَّعْتَرَ وَ كَانَ يَقُولُ إِنَّهُ يَصِيرُ لِلْمَعِدَةِ خَمْلًا كَخَمْلِ الْقَطِيفَةِ .

segments: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Ziyad Al Qandy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> the 1st having said: ‘The medicine of Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> was the thyme, and he<sup>asws</sup> was saying: ‘It comes to the stomach as velvet like the plush brocade’.
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Ziyad Al Qandy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> the 1st having said: ‘The medicine of Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> was the thyme, and he<sup>asws</sup> was saying: ‘It comes to the stomach as velvet like the plush brocade’.

segments: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Ziyad al-Qandiy who has said the following: “Abu al-Hassan, al-Awwal, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that the medicine of ’Amir al-Mu’minin was wild thyme, ’Amir al-Mu’minin would say, Thyme becomes fuzz and fluff for the stomach like that of velvet.’”
en.sarwar.text: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Ziyad al-Qandiy who has said the following: “Abu al-Hassan, al-Awwal, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that the medicine of ’Amir al-Mu’minin was wild thyme, ’Amir al-Mu’minin would say, Thyme becomes fuzz and fluff for the stomach like that of velvet.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/222)
gradings.label: موثق
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/222)

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ زِيَادٍ الْقَنْدِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ الْأَوَّلِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

segments: أَنَّهُ شَكَا إِلَيْهِ رُطُوبَةً فَأَمَرَهُ أَنْ يَسْتَفَّ السَّعْتَرَ عَلَى الرِّيقِ .
arabic.text: أَنَّهُ شَكَا إِلَيْهِ رُطُوبَةً فَأَمَرَهُ أَنْ يَسْتَفَّ السَّعْتَرَ عَلَى الرِّيقِ .

segments: From him, from Musa Bin Al Hassan, from Ali Bin Suleyman, from one of the middle-men,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, that he complained to him<sup>asws</sup> of moisture (acidity), so he<sup>asws</sup> ordered him that he should eat the thyme upon the empty stomach’.
en.hubeali.text:
From him, from Musa Bin Al Hassan, from Ali Bin Suleyman, from one of the middle-men,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, that he complained to him<sup>asws</sup> of moisture (acidity), so he<sup>asws</sup> ordered him that he should eat the thyme upon the empty stomach’.

segments: 2. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from Musa ibn al-Hassan from Ali ibn Sulayman from certain ones of al-Wasit who has said the following: “I once complained before abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about rutubah (acidity) and he (the Imam) commanded me to eat thyme complete and uncrushed.”
en.sarwar.text: 2. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from Musa ibn al-Hassan from Ali ibn Sulayman from certain ones of al-Wasit who has said the following: “I once complained before abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about rutubah (acidity) and he (the Imam) commanded me to eat thyme complete and uncrushed.”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/222)
gradings.label: مجهول
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/222)

narratorChain.raw: عَنْهُ عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ عَنْ بَعْضِ الْوَاسِطِيِّينَ عَنْ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام )
